stage.name: The Crypto World (Elementary)
stage.description: Beyond Bitcoin — NFTs, DAOs, Web3, and crypto in real life.

elements.content: Crypto isn't just money — it's a whole ecosystem

When most people hear 'crypto' they think of Bitcoin. But the crypto world is actually massive — like how the internet isn't just email. There are games, art, banking, social media, and entire economies all built on blockchain. Here's a quick tour.

🎮

Gaming

Play-to-earn — own your in-game items as NFTs

🗳️

DAOs

Groups that vote on decisions using blockchain

🌐

Web3 Apps

Apps where you own your own data

actions.text: 🌍 The Crypto World
Crypto isn't just money — it's a whole ecosystem

When most people hear 'crypto' they think of Bitcoin. But the crypto world is actually massive — like how the internet isn't just email. There are games, art, banking, social media, and entire economies all built on blockchain. Here's a quick tour.
🎮 Gaming Play-to-earn — own your in-game items as NFTs
🖼️ NFTs Unique digital ownership certificates
🗳️ DAOs Groups that vote on decisions using blockchain
🌐 Web3 Apps Apps where you own your own data

elements.content: NFT = Non-Fungible Token. 'Non-fungible' means one of a kind. An NFT is a digital certificate on the blockchain that proves you own something unique. A $1 bill is fungible (swap it for any other $1). The Mona Lisa is non-fungible — there's only one original. NFTs make digital things work like the Mona Lisa.

🎵

Music

Artists sell song originals directly to fans

⚽

Sports

Highlight clips owned as NFTs (NBA Top Shot)

actions.text: 🖼️ What is an NFT?

NFT = Non-Fungible Token. 'Non-fungible' means one of a kind . An NFT is a digital certificate on the blockchain that proves you own something unique. A $1 bill is fungible (swap it for any other $1). The Mona Lisa is non-fungible — there's only one original. NFTs make digital things work like the M...
🎵 Music Artists sell song originals directly to fans
⚽ Sports Highlight clips owned as NFTs (NBA Top Shot)
🎮 Gaming Own and resell your game characters
🖼️ Art Digital art sold for millions

elements.content: Two types of exchanges — what is the difference?

CEX (Centralized Exchange) — a company runs it. Think Coinbase, Binance, Kraken. You create an account, verify your identity, and the company holds your funds while you trade. Easy to use but you depend on them.


DEX (Decentralized Exchange) — no company. Code runs it. You connect your own wallet and trade directly. Nobody holds your funds. Examples: Uniswap, dYdX, XRPL's built-in DEX.

actions.text: 🏛️ CEX vs DEX
Two types of exchanges — what is the difference?

CEX (Centralized Exchange) — a company runs it. Think Coinbase, Binance, Kraken. You create an account, verify your identity, and the company holds your funds while you trade. Easy to use but you depend on them. DEX (Decentralized Exchange) — no company. Code runs it. You connect your own wallet and trade directly. Nobody holds your funds. Examples: Uniswap, dYdX, XRPL's built-in DEX.
🏦 CEX. Company-run, ID required
🔄 DEX. Code-run, wallet-only
✅ CEX Pros. Easy, beginner-friendly
✅ DEX Pros. Self-custody, no accounts

elements.content: How crypto enables groups to govern themselves

A DAO (Decentralized Autonomous Organization) is like a company where the rules are written in code and the members vote on decisions using their tokens.


No CEO. No board. Every token holder gets a vote proportional to how many tokens they hold. Decisions — like how to spend the treasury or change the protocol — are voted on-chain and executed automatically.

actions.text: 🖼️ DAOs — Ownership by the Community
How crypto enables groups to govern themselves

A DAO (Decentralized Autonomous Organization) is like a company where the rules are written in code and the members vote on decisions using their tokens. No CEO. No board. Every token holder gets a vote proportional to how many tokens they hold. Decisions — like how to spend the treasury or change the protocol — are voted on-chain and executed automatically.
🗳️ Token Vote. Hold tokens = voting power
📋 On-Chain Rules. Code enforces the result
🌐 Examples. MakerDAO, Uniswap DAO
💡 Key Idea. Community = the company

elements.content: What Web3 means and why it matters

Web1 — read-only. Static websites. You could read, not participate.


Web2 — read-write. Social media. You can create — but companies (Meta, Google) own your data and earn from it.


Web3 — read-write-own. You create content and own it via blockchain. Your identity, data, and digital assets belong to you — not a platform.

🚀 Now
Crypto is the foundation of Web3

actions.text: 🌐 Web3 — The Next Internet
What Web3 means and why it matters

Web1 — read-only. Static websites. You could read, not participate. Web2 — read-write. Social media. You can create — but companies (Meta, Google) own your data and earn from it. Web3 — read-write-own. You create content and own it via blockchain . Your identity, data, and digital assets belong to you — not a platform.
📖 Web1. Read only — static sites
✍️ Web2. Create but platform owns data
🔑 Web3. Own your data and assets
🚀 Now. Crypto is the foundation of Web3

questions.question: What does NFT stand for?
questions.analysis: NFT stands for Non-Fungible Token. 'Non-fungible' means unique or one-of-a-kind — unlike Bitcoin where every coin is identical and interchangeable.

questions.question: What is a DAO?

options.label: A group that makes decisions together using blockchain votes — no CEO needed

questions.analysis: A DAO (Decentralized Autonomous Organization) is a group that votes on decisions using blockchain. Every vote is transparent and the code automatically carries out what the majority decides.

questions.question: Which country made Bitcoin legal tender — meaning you can pay for groceries with it?
questions.analysis: El Salvador became the first country to make Bitcoin legal tender in 2021. Citizens can use Bitcoin to pay for anything — from groceries to utility bills.

questions.question: What makes an NFT different from a regular digital file?

options.label: NFTs can only be used in video games
NFTs use blockchain to prove ownership — a regular file can be copied infinitely with no proof of original ownership
NFTs are stored on a USB drive
NFTs are controlled by the government

questions.analysis: Anyone can save a copy of a JPEG, but an NFT on the blockchain proves who owns the original. It's the difference between a photocopy of the Mona Lisa and owning the actual painting.

actions.text: Time to test what you've learned! Answer each question and see how you do.
Take your time — the goal is understanding, not just getting the right answer.
